channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: GitLab has patched a host of worrying security issues
item.description: Among the problems were two critical severity issues which allowed threat actors to bypass authentication.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Commerce secretary says Trump wants TikTok deal done under current deadline
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick on Friday said President Trump would like to hammer out a deal on TikTok's future ownership under the current deadline, saying the president "doesn't like to ask for extensions." "So, it's in Donald Trump's hands.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Volt Typhoon threat group had access to American utility networks for the best part of a year
item.description: 2023 saw the Chinese threat group Volt Typhoon get access to public power and water utilities in Massachusetts for 10 months.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: Apple releases emergency security update for serious vulnerability
item.description: A newly discovered security flaw with your iPhone or iPad could leave your personal information vulnerable to hackers. Kurt the CyberGuy explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple plans AirPods feature that can live-translate conversations
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- Apple Inc. is planning a new AirPods feature that allows the earbuds to live-translate an in-person conversation into another language, according to people with knowledge of the matter.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: A blueprint for effective cloud recovery
item.description: As businesses increasingly rely on multi-cloud environments, building cloud resilience becomes critical. In this article, we will explore the key components of cloud resilience and its importance in recovering from outages such as ransomware attacks.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CAIOs are stepping out from the CIO’s shadow
item.description: In 2016, Andrew Ng, one of the best-known researchers in the field of AI, wrote about the benefits of establishing a chief AI officer role in companies, as well as the characteristics and responsibilities such a role should have.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tech stocks tumble as AI boom fizzles out
item.description: The world’s biggest technology companies have seen their stock prices tumble over the past month as wider tumult in the market hits the tech sector hard after years of steep gains driven by artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Intel under Tan: What enterprise IT buyers need to know
item.description: Intel’s appointment of semiconductor veteran Lip-Bu Tan as CEO marks a critical moment for the company and its enterprise customers. With rising competition from AMD, Arm-based chips, and RISC-V alternatives, Intel faces mounting pressure to defend its x86 dominance.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Robotaxis are racking up tickets in San Francisco
item.description: San Francisco is a hot spot for Waymo vehicles, which operate without a human driver, but it seems the cars don't always play by the rules.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: What is Llama? Meta AI’s family of large language models explained
item.description: Llama, a family of sort-of open-source large language models (LLMs), was introduced in February 2023, and has been updated periodically ever since. Llama shook up the world of LLMs, which had previously been dominated by ever-larger closed-source models from OpenAI and Google.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Java Shops Adjust Plans Amid Economic Uncertainty
item.description: A report by Perforce reveals shifts in Java development plans, AI tool adoption, and tech stack preferences among surveyed developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Postponed NASA, Space X Falcon 9 mission rescheduled for Friday
item.description: NASA and SpaceX rescheduled the launch of their Falcon 9 rocket rescue mission to the International Space Station for Friday. They were unable to launch on Wednesday due to issues with equipment on the ground, and opted to scrub a Thursday launch attempt due to weather.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Mozilla: Update Firefox immediately
item.description: Expired certificates have recently caused a lot of chaos, including for Chromecast users. With that in mind, Mozilla is now urging all Firefox users to immediately update the browser to the latest version.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Better data, not a review, new systems will fix acquisition
item.description: President Donald Trump is charging DOGE and agencies to look at contracting processes, procedures and the systems that support those efforts by late March. The post Better data, not a review, new systems will fix acquisition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence
item.description: The U.S. Copyright Office completed the second of two studies of AI and copyrights as it dealt with whether you can copyright outputs created using AI. The post The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Weaviate adds agents to its tech stack to ease gen AI app development
item.description: Vector database provider Weaviate has added three new agents to its development stack to facilitate the development of generative AI-based applications. These agents are modular agentic workflows that use large language models (LLMs) and prompts to interact dynamically with data in Weaviate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New delivery robot can haul 2,200 pounds of your stuff
item.description: WeRide's unmanned electric delivery van, the Robovan W5, has 194 cubic feet of cargo space and can carry payloads up to about 2,205 pounds
item.dc:creator: Fox News
